description: Bacterial flagellar assembly is a hierarchical developmental program involving ~50 genes organized into three transcriptional classes (early, middle, late) controlled by the FlhDC master regulator. The system demonstrates temporal gene expression with checkpoint control: early genes build the basal body, middle genes create the hook, and late genes produce the filament. Assembly order is enforced by anti-sigma factors and substrate specificity switching in the type III secretion system. This represents one of nature's most complex molecular machines.

name: Flagellar Assembly and FlhDC Regulation
notes: 62 nodes showing hierarchical flagellar assembly program. Contains 3 OR gates (environment check, hook length, motor function) and 1 AND gate (basal body completion requires multiple components). Demonstrates checkpoint control with hook-length sensor FliK triggering substrate specificity switch. Shows all three gene classes and assembly order enforcement.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on verified genetic and structural data from decades of flagellar research. The hierarchical regulatory cascade and assembly checkpoints are well-established. All gene classes, regulatory proteins, and assembly order have been validated against primary research sources.
